Details: Ozone was found to be quite effective in oxidizing the contaminant in pilot-scale work. A dose of 1 mg/L ozone achieved 60% removal, while increasing the dose to 2.9 mg/L achieved > 99% removal. Contact times for the study were just over 7 minutes. One study found complete removal of glyphosate at applied ozone dose of 1.9 mg/L with 7 minute contact time.
